steps.title: Combine the two equations
steps.content: Substitute one equation into the other to get a single quadratic in $x$. The system has no real solutions exactly when that quadratic has no real solutions — i.e., when the discriminant $b^{2} - 4ac \lt 0$.

steps.title: Solve the discriminant inequality
steps.content:
Setting up $b^{2} - 4ac \lt 0$ and simplifying produces an inequality in $a$. Solving for $a$ shows the smallest positive integer value of $a$ that makes the discriminant negative is $a = 16$.

The answer is 16.
